10.reason: [Spaces in the expression are not ignored; this reads a space character as an operator, causing incorrect parsing. Need to skip whitespace before reading flag2.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Spaces in the expression are not ignored; this reads a space character as an operator, causing incorrect parsing. Need to skip whitespace before reading flag2.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: This line reads the next character without skipping any whitespace. After scanf("%d") reads a number, a space or newline may be left in the input buffer. The program will then incorrectly treat that whitespace as an operator (flag2). This leads to misinterpreting the expression, especially when spaces are present. The correct approach is to ignore leading whitespace before reading the operator, e.g., using scanf(" %c", &flag2).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  scanf("%c") does not skip whitespace, causing it to read spaces as operators. Use " %c" to skip leading whitespace.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The %c format specifier does not skip whitespace characters. It will read spaces as operators, causing the subsequent scanf("%d") to fail and leading to incorrect parsing or undefined behavior. It should be " %c" to skip leading spaces.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This reads any character including whitespace; it may read a space or newline left in the input buffer instead of the actual operator. This causes incorrect parsing of the expression.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This reads any character including whitespace. It may read a space left in the input buffer after reading an integer, causing incorrect operator detection.]
